architectReview.evidence: Architecture: KEY decision — engine write actions already own the atomic reserve->dispatch->finalize guardrail sequence, so guardrail-bridge does NOT re-reserve (avoids double-counting rate/idempotency and preserving atomicity). The bridge maps tool params (dryRun default TRUE, cap clamped to 20, interactive:false) and classifies engine result envelopes; the engine action is the single dispatch path. cloak_provider_write shares the fail-closed resolution + a per-provider WRITE allowlist with the read tool. cloak_credentials delegates to redacted-only reads; reveal is structurally impossible (no cleartext API) and returns needs-confirmation. Product: dryRun default performs no navigation; unknown/read/helper actions refused; duplicate/concurrent reservations admit exactly one; credential reveal host-only. Code: single PROVIDERS table for reads+writes, single bridge, no duplication; imports guardrails/credentials via deep engine subpaths; engine unchanged.
commands: leader architect review over G005 changeset (guardrail-bridge.mjs, providers write tool + writes allowlist, credentials tool, index/catalog, phase4 tests)

executorQa.evidence: dryRun-default proven to perform NO navigation at the tool boundary; unknown-provider and read-as-write refused; duplicate + concurrent guarded reservations admit exactly one (no double dispatch); credential reveal returns needs-confirmation and list returns redacted profiles. Full mcp suite (40) green; engine guardrails invariants (16) green and unaffected.

iteration.evidence: Leader ai-slop-cleaner sweep on the G005 changeset: no blocking findings. Key correctness call caught during the sweep — NOT re-reserving in the bridge (engine already self-guards) prevents double-counting rate/idempotency; verified engine write actions self-guard via source (resolveWriteGate/reserveGuardedAction/finalizeGuardedAction across all provider write modules). No duplication (one PROVIDERS table, one bridge), no dead code, no fallback masking. Full suite rerun clean.
